---
title: "Exploring Storms"
author: "Your Name"
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(tidyverse))

#| label: storm-plot
#| warning: false
#| message: false

# Process the data to count storms by type per year
storms_per_year <- storms %>%
  filter(status %in% c("tropical depression", "tropical storm", "hurricane")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(name)) %>%
  group_by(year, status) %>%
  summarize(count = n_distinct(name), .groups = "drop")

# Define a custom color scale for the storm types
storm_colors <- c(
  "tropical depression" = "#89CFF0",
  "tropical storm" = "#FFA500",
  "hurricane" = "#8B0000"
)

# Create the stacked area chart
ggplot(storms_per_year, aes(x = year, y = count, fill = status)) +
  geom_area(position = "stack", alpha = 0.85) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = storm_colors) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Storm Counts by Type per Year",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Count of Storms",
    fill = "Storm Type"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
